reviewer.name: OpenAI Codex
reviewer.responsibility: Claim extraction, primary-source mapping, scope correction, and verification design

sources.title: HTML input element
sources.versionScope: Current HTML standard and evergreen browsers

claims.statement: The input element creates a form control whose behavior depends on its type.
Input attributes such as type, name, value, required, and constraints affect interaction and submission.
Not all input types are user-editable text fields, and unnamed controls are not submitted normally.

verifications.method: Enter a known value and confirm the application reads that exact value.
verifications.expectedResult: The application reads that exact value.

verifications.method: Check the empty or invalid-input path used by the surrounding form.
verifications.expectedResult: The check exposes whether input behaves as documented: Check the empty or invalid-input path used by the surrounding form.

verifications.method: Confirm the label, name, type, and code reference describe the same value.
verifications.expectedResult: The label, name, type, and code reference describe the same value.

limitations: Not every input type accepts typed text; inputs also include controls such as checkbox, radio, file, hidden, and submit.
A control generally needs a name to contribute a name/value pair during form submission.
Labels and suitable input types are important accessibility and data-quality boundaries.
Independent human review remains required before promotion to gold.
